spec_match.rationale: The page now exercises the real @font-face font-feature-settings descriptor through CSSFontFaceRule.style.fontFeatureSettings, not just an element-level simulation. The descriptor lab parses valid alias descriptors, normal, disabled values, and malformed tags with CSSStyleSheet.replaceSync().

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the family selector buttons and descriptor-mode buttons. The selected alias changed the sample font-family and CSSOM rule readout; normal, liga off, and malformed tag modes updated the status cards and generated CSS.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still matches the feature-index promise of named font aliases with baked-in feature tags, and now also makes the v139 motivation visible by contrasting descriptor reuse with pre-v139 per-element repetition.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces valid descriptor retention, normal keyword handling, off-to-0 normalization, malformed feature-tag rejection, and a visible fallback if constructed stylesheets are unavailable.

references.rationale: The concept page links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5102801981800448 and the CSS Fonts 4 font rendering descriptor section.

design_system.rationale: The new controls use shared CSS variables, set color whenever button backgrounds change, avoid raw hex colors, and keep the existing compact demo layout.

summary: Pre-fix critique identified missing ChromeStatus linkage, simulated descriptor behavior, and no edge/failure branch. Post-fix DevTools MCP testing in Chrome 148 at http://localhost:3000/v139/support-font-feature-settings-descriptor-in-font-face-rule/font-alias-variants/ clicked Small Caps, Combined, normal, liga off, and malformed tag controls. The page reported CSSFontFaceRule parsing, retained valid descriptor tags, normalized liga off to "liga" 0, and dropped the malformed "ligature" descriptor. Console was clean and local page/style requests returned 200. The feature conformance route at /v139/support-font-feature-settings-descriptor-in-font-face-rule/conformance/ reported 6 PASS, 0 FAIL.
